focus: Driver abandonment, strong 3-wood alternatives, fairway accuracy vs distance trade-off, mini driver solutions

PAIN.quote: I have been battling the last 6 months with my tee club. As my irons and fairway woods improved, my driver game deteriorated. I have tried every variation of driver possible including a mini driver. Everything is extremely inconsistent
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

PAIN.quote: Shotscope data had me at only about 33% fairways hit. When I miss, I miss bad. I'm talking that I might only get 100-150 yards off the tee bad which has been leaving me 200+ yards on my second shot on almost 2/3 of my par 4s
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

PAIN.quote: I've stopped using my driver and strictly use a strong 3 wood/2 wood off the tee. Over the years I've gone through countless drivers. I've been fitted and taken lessons. But, I've never been consistent with my driver
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood
PAIN.context: Countless drivers, fittings, lessons - never consistent

PAIN.quote: I have yet to find a driver I can't match with my 3 wood. I'm still trying with fittings and such, but a new PXG gen4 XF I have is only getting 240 or so yards. I can do 235 with my 15 degree 3 wood
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver
PAIN.context: New driver only 5 yards longer than 3 wood

PAIN.quote: I'm a mini driver user, I make a fool of myself with 460 CC head. Get the ball in play is my goal
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver
PAIN.context: Makes fool of self with driver - needs ball in play

PAIN.quote: My preferred shot shape is a draw which I find difficult with the current drivers. Combined with a steepish swing it's a struggle. A couple of friends convinced me to try and hit a fade with driver, which has helped a bit
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

PAIN.quote: The geometry of the modern driver pulls a large amount of mass off the face. Some players have delivery characteristics that do not jive well with these designs. They may end up ballooning the ball, having excessive spin rates or other undesirable launch conditions
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver
PAIN.context: Modern driver geometry doesn't work for everyone

HOPE.quote: In the last 3 rounds, I'm 80% on fairways with a total yardage of around 235 yards. The 20% of the time I'm off the fairway, it has been manageable except for 1 yank into the water which is the only ball I've lost over the last 3 rounds
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

HOPE.quote: Recently I've been using a strong 3 wood/2 wood off the tee and it's so accurate and consistent. It's easy to hit and my fairway percentages have increased dramatically. On average I lost 10-15 yards but that's if I hit my driver perfect
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

HOPE.quote: I go back and forth between my 3w and my driver as my top club. One thing I have noticed is I get in a lot less trouble with my 3w than with my driver and that makes my round a lot more fun. What distance I lose, which isn't much, is made up easily with more fairways instead of rough or worse
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

HOPE.quote: I've been rocking a SLDR mini since it came out, and have zero regrets in using it as my main tee club. Accuracy can never hurt either and if you are comfortable with some longer approaches in some cases, it equals out
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

HOPE.quote: So, my scores have dropped considerably in just the 3 rounds since I've made the change. This has mostly been attributed to having 7 wood or lower in my hands for second shot with the strong 3 wood
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

HOPE.quote: The 17 degree Callaway Super Hybrid will make you question whether or not you need a driver or three wood. It is LONG and forgiving
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood
HOPE.context: Super Hybrid questions need for driver

HOPE.quote: Gladly give up some yardage for 3-4 more fairways on a course that's really punishing
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The issue is that most don't realize that the driver is the outlier in the bag that is struck at a different point along the swing arc than the rest of the bag. Every club in the bag that you hit off the ground is struck with a descending blow
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I assume that this is most likely a mental thing as I have to go from a positive angle of attack on the driver to my more natural negative angle of attack on the other clubs. When I do get a good drive off, carry is about 230 yards with a roll out to about 250 yards
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Positive vs negative angle of attack mental challenge

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Trying to hit the ball on the up produces the wildest block slices. I tee the ball down now which makes my driver swing a bit more like it is with the rest of my clubs. It's made a huge difference
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: My thing is I tend to over swing with my driver which as noted above is more between the ears than anything else
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Anytime the thought over a shot is to hit it as far as possible, trouble becomes more likely. With every other club in the bag, we accept not only limited yardages, but a VERY specific target. All too often with driver, rather than a VERY small target, there is no target except FAR
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I took my driver out of the bag to avoid temptation, and I notched my Mizuno GT-180 3 wood down to 13 degrees
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: If you hit your 3 wood consistently, then you might consider cutting your driver length down. I'm playing a 44 inch Mizuno ST-190G. Now it's just an inch longer than a typical 3 wood. If you're consistent with a 3 wood, this should definitely improve your consistency with the driver
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I've had a degree of success with shortening the driver and adding some weight to it to add back some feel and to slow down my tempo
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: You could always just keep the driver and only use it on wide open holes and get a strong 3 wood for everything else. That's the way I'm leaning
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I was about to do the same, ditch my driver for my 3w - 230-240's club… then I got on trackman and found out what my issue was. I didn't even need to buy a new driver. I just needed to get my numbers dialed in
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: It might not be the loft as much as overall playing length of the strong 3 wood. Normal length is 43 inches. Back in the day, my driver with steel shaft was 43 inches
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Swing speed plays a big role. Sub 95 mph, go with 44 inch/ 14 degrees. Could be labeled a 3 wood or driver
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The ping g425 max started at 14.5 and lofts down twice. It's a very solid 3w. Just remember that if you choose to deloft then you're opening the face and inviting a hard cut/slice
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Try and fix your swing and/or get fitted for driver. In the meantime mini driver is 100% your best option. Bigger face than 3 wood so it's more forgiving off the tee
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Low Loft 3 Wood Instead Of Driver
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Mini driver = bigger face = more forgiving

VILLAIN.quote: I played a three wood off the tee for awhile, until I did it in some tournaments and realized what I was giving up relative to other players. 10-15 yds. is a LOT, and there is just no way that hitting a club or two more into 14 greens is a good long-term recipe
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

VILLAIN.quote: When I started playing again I used an X Hot 3 Deep for about a year off the tee. However, I found that the worst 5%-10% of shots with this club were actually worse than the worst 5%-10% of shots with a driver when you considered both accuracy and distance lost
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood
VILLAIN.context: 3 wood bad shots worse than driver bad shots

VILLAIN.quote: Hitting the driver and hoping for that long straight bomb is just too much fun to give up. In my group we like to compare equipment, bust chops over drives good and bad
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood

VILLAIN.quote: I think having a driver in the bag and confidence with it on the course is important to shooting your best possible score. It might be a 44 inch 13 degree driver... but I think you need to find a way to maximize length while minimizing risk
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - I ditched my driver for a strong 3 wood
VILLAIN.context: Need driver confidence for best score

VILLAIN.quote: 30-50+ yards depending on the conditions and how I'm swinging as well. At 15 yards that isn't much for sure but for me if having a short iron in vs a 5i or 6i that definitely changes the scores quickly
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Strong 3 Wood for me - no more Driver

high_value_themes_identified: 33% to 80% fairways by switching to 3 wood
Modern driver geometry doesn't work for everyone
Driver swing fundamentally different - mental challenge
Overswinging driver is between the ears
Sub-95 mph needs 44 inch 14 degree club
